title: Cynorkis breviplectra
intro: Cynorkis breviplectra, also known as the short-tongued Cynorkis, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Cynorkis breviplectra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cynorkis breviplectra is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cynorkis, a group of flowering plants commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is further categorized as being part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes many other types of orchids. Cynorkis breviplectra is native to the tropical regions of Madagascar, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats.
